content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 11, 1994

Honorable Sam P. Karas
Supervisor, District 5
County of Monterey
Monterey Courthouse
1200 Aguajito Road, Suite 001
Monterey, CA 93940

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-94-211

Dear Supervisor Karas:

This is in response to your request for assistance regarding
the Political Reform Act.¹ Since your advice request does not
refer to a specific governmental decision, we are treating your
request as one for informal assistance.²

Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed
to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place. In
addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us. The
Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC ops. 71.)

QUESTION

Does Section 84308 prohibit you, as member of the California
Coastal Commission, from making decisions affecting a person who
contributed more than $250 to your campaign for the Monterey
County Board of Supervisors?

___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000 - 18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-94-211
Page 2


CONCLUSION

You may not participate in any coastal commission decision
involving a license, permit or other entitlement for use where you
have received a contribution of more than $250 within the
preceding 12 months from a party or his or her agent, or from any
participant, or his or her agent. In addition, you must disclose
that fact on the record of the proceeding.

FACTS

You are a member of the Monterey County Board of Supervisors
and a member of the California Coastal Commission. In January
1994, you received a contribution from Anthony Lombardo, an
attorney who represents clients before the coastal commission.
The contribution was given to your campaign account for the board
of supervisors and was in excess of $250.

ANALYSIS

In 1983, Section 84308 was added to the Act in order to
ensure that appointed members of boards or commissions would not
be biased by contributions or potential contributors of large
campaign contributions who might appear before them in a
proceeding involving a license, permit or entitlement for use.
Section 84308 applies to all appointed officers of any state
agency or local government agency, with the exception of the
courts or any agency in the judicial branch of government, the
Legislature, the Board of Equalization, and constitutional
officers. (Section 84308(a)(3).) This would include persons
appointed to the California Coastal Commission. (Dickey Advice
Letter, No. I-88-232.)

Section 84308 imposes two requirements on officers subject to
the section. First, the officer may accept, solicits 3 or direct
contribution of more than $250, from any party,4 or his or her
agent, or from any participant, or his or her agent, while a
proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for
use is pending before the agency and for three months following
the date a final decision is rendered in the proceeding. (Section
84308(b).)


3 This prohibition applies regardless of whether the officer
accepts, solicits, or directs the contribution for himself or
herself, or on behalf of any other officer, on behalf of any
candidate for office or on behalf of any committee.

4 "Party" means any person who files an application for, or is
the subject of, a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other
entitlement for use. (Section 84308(a)(1).)

Our File No. I-94-211
Page 3


     Furthermore, if an officer has received5 a contribution of
more than $250 during the past 12 months from a party participant in a proceeding involving a license, permit or other
entitlement for use pending before an agency, the officer must
disclose that fact on the record of the proceeding and must
disqualify himself or herself from participating.  (Section
84308(c).)

     You have asked about the application of Section 84308 to an
attorney who represents parties before the Coastal Commission.
Regulation 18438.3(b) requires that in determining whether a
contribution has been made for purposes of Section 84308 by a
person or his or her agent, contributions made by that person
within the preceding 12 months must be aggregated with those made
by his or her agent within the preceding 12 months or the period
of the agency relationship, whichever is shorter.

     Regulation 18438.3(a) also provides that a person is the
"agent" of a party to, or a participant in, a proceeding involving
a license, permit, or other entitlement for use only if he or she
represents that person in connection with the proceeding involving
the license, permit, or other entitlement for use.6

     Since Mr. Lombardo will be representing clients before the
coastal commission, his contributions are to be cumulated with any
contributions made by his clients.  In this case, since
Mr. Lombardo has made a contribution in excess of $250, you would
be required to disqualify yourself even where the client made no
contributions.

     However, please note Section 84308(c) provides that an
official may participate in the proceeding if he or she returns
the contribution (or amount of the contribution in excess of $250)


_________________________

     5 Regulation 18438.6(a) provides that for purposes of Section
84308, a person "accepts" or "receives" a contribution only if the
contribution is for that person's own candidacy or own controlled
committee.

     6 If an individual acting as an agent is also acting as an
employee or member of a law, architectural, engineering, or
consulting firm, or a similar entity or corporation, both the
entity or corporation and the individual are "agents."  However,
contributions of other firm members (Burt Advice Memorandum,
T-83-042) or clerical or secretarial employees of the firm would not be
cumulated with the attorney's contributions absent reimbursement
or control by the attorney or firm.  (Bagatelos Advice Letter, No.
I-89-696.)

Our File No. I-94-211
Page 4


within 30 days from the time he or she knows, or should have
known, both of the following:

     1.  About the receipt of the contribution; and,

     2.  About the proceeding involving a license, permit, or
other entitlement for use.  (See, Regulation 18438.7, copy
enclosed.)

I trust these general guidelines will assist you.  If you
have any further questions regarding this matter or a specific
decision for which you need advice, please feel free to contact me
at (916) 322-5901.7


                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                             [signature]
                                        By: John W. Wallace
                                            Counsel, Legal Division




7
     Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
